175.reason: [Error: If k is NULL (i.e., G[j].link is NULL), dereferencing k->adjvex causes undefined behavior. This can happen if vertex j has no edges or after deleting the vertex itself.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: If k is NULL (i.e., G[j].link is NULL), dereferencing k->adjvex causes undefined behavior. This can happen if vertex j has no edges or after deleting the vertex itself.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Potential null pointer dereference: if G[j].link is NULL (e.g., vertex j has no edges), then k is NULL and accessing k->adjvex causes undefined behavior. The code does not check for NULL before this line.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.5 , reason:  Potential NULL pointer dereference: if G[j].link is NULL, k is NULL and accessing k->adjvex causes undefined behavior. Although the problem guarantees connectivity, this is a logical error.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Missing NULL check; if G[j].link is NULL, dereferencing k causes segmentation fault.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.5 , reason:  Potential NULL pointer dereference if G[j].link is NULL
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  k could be NULL if G[j].link is NULL, causing a segmentation fault

172.reason: The vertex del's own adjacency list is not freed, meaning the vertex is not completely removed from the graph. This is an error because the vertex should be deleted entirely.
